generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helper variables in AGREE expressions.
Ensure AGREE type declarations (int, real, bool) are used correctly.
Focus on fixing unresolved references or invalid helper declarations reported by the AGREE validator.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new AADL architecture to resolve AGREE validation errors.
Avoid placing guarantee statements within implementation annexes.
Do not attempt to fix type mismatches by altering the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL scope rules for component types and implementations
Placement of assumptions and guarantees in component type vs. implementation scopes
